snippet: Clause 12 of Ministerial Order 706 (PDF) External Link sets out the minimum requirements for anaphylaxis management training in schools that have students enrolled who are at risk of anaphylaxis and the guidelines provide further details on the training requirements. Schools have 2 options for staff training: The department recommends that all Victorian school staff undertake Option 1 which: requires that staff show the school anaphylaxis supervisor that they can competently use an adrenaline device to successfully complete the course. This capability must be tested within 30 days of completing the online training course. Who is required to undertake anaphylaxis management training? The Order specifies that school staff must undertake training in anaphylaxis management if they: Schools are encouraged to consider whether volunteers at the school and regular casual relief teachers should also undertake training.
